title: clinopodium alpinum subsp. dinaricum
intro: Clinopodium alpinum subsp. dinaricum, also known as Dinaric mountain clinopodium, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the Balkan Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Clinopodium alpinum subsp. dinaricum include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, reduce fever, and support digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional folk medicine.
